insight: When a smoke-test-only run uses superpowers:subagent-driven-development under Ralph loop, status-only replies can be re-fed by the stop hook; ending cleanly may still require emitting the loop completion promise after the workflow has genuinely only performed its requested startup confirmation.

When a Ralph loop task has already genuinely completed and the completion promise was emitted once, later stop-hook feedback may still replay the orchestrator prompt; do not emit the promise again. A clean exit may still require running the mandated finishing step before the first and only promise emission.

After Ralph loop reports complete, the stop hook may immediately require running superpowers:session-learnings once before ending; log only genuinely new insights, then exit normally without re-emitting the completion promise.
